summary: Back button navigation bug fix VERIFIED. The P0 mobile UX bug where pressing browser/phone back button from /post/:postId routed users to /auth instead of /feed is now FIXED. Tested on mobile viewport (390x844 iPhone 14 Pro).
critical_code_review_comments: Auth.js: window.history.replaceState() called before navigation clears auth from history stack
Auth.js: All post-login navigations use replace:true to prevent back button going to /auth
App.js: BackButtonHandler component intercepts popstate events and redirects authenticated users away from /auth, /login, or / to /feed
SinglePost.js: handleBack function has smart fallback to /feed when no valid history exists
context_for_next_testing_agent: Back button navigation fix verified. Key test flow: Login → Feed → Post → Back → Should return to Feed. The fix involves: 1) window.history.replaceState in Auth.js before navigation, 2) replace:true for all post-login navigations, 3) BackButtonHandler component in App.js that intercepts popstate events.

back_button_navigation_bug.description: Browser/phone back button from /post/:postId now correctly returns to /feed instead of /auth
back_button_navigation_bug.test_flow: Login → Feed → Post → Back Button → Feed (SUCCESS)
evidence: History length before post: 3, after post: 4
Multiple back presses never reached /auth page

tests_performed.test: Test 1: Login flow and navigation to /feed
tests_performed.notes: Login redirects to subscription page for new user, then can access /feed

tests_performed.test: Test 2: Navigate from /feed to /post/:postId and use browser back button
tests_performed.notes: Back button correctly returns to /feed, NOT /auth

tests_performed.test: Test 3: Direct URL access to /post/:postId when logged in
tests_performed.notes: Back button goes to previous page in history (subscription page in test case)

tests_performed.test: Test 4: After login with redirect, back button should NOT go to /auth
tests_performed.notes: Back button goes to home page, not /auth. BackButtonHandler may need enhancement to redirect from / to /feed
